howItWorks.intro: Aspect ratio describes the proportional relationship between width and height. It's essential for photography, video, web design, and print layout.
rules: Find Ratio mode: enter width and height to discover the simplified aspect ratio
Scale by Ratio mode: pick a target ratio and one dimension to calculate the other
The calculator simplifies ratios using the greatest common divisor (GCD)
howItWorks.outro: Common aspect ratios include 16:9 (HD video and modern monitors), 4:3 (classic TV and iPad), 3:2 (35mm photography), and 1:1 (Instagram square).

faq.question: What is aspect ratio?
faq.answer: Aspect ratio is the proportional relationship between the width and height of an image or screen. It's expressed as two numbers separated by a colon, like 16:9. A 16:9 ratio means for every 16 units of width, there are 9 units of height.

faq.question: What aspect ratio is 1920x1080?
faq.answer: 1920x1080 is a 16:9 aspect ratio, also known as Full HD (1080p). This is the most common aspect ratio for modern monitors, TVs, and video content. Other 16:9 resolutions include 1280x720 (720p), 2560x1440 (1440p), and 3840x2160 (4K).

faq.question: What is the best aspect ratio for social media?
faq.answer: It depends on the platform. Instagram posts use 1:1 (square) or 4:5 (portrait). Instagram Stories and TikTok use 9:16 (vertical). YouTube and most video use 16:9 (widescreen). Facebook posts work well with 1.91:1. Twitter images are best at 16:9 or 2:1.

faq.question: How do I calculate aspect ratio from pixel dimensions?
faq.answer: Divide both width and height by their greatest common divisor (GCD). For example, 1920÷120 = 16 and 1080÷120 = 9, giving you 16:9. This calculator does this automatically for any dimensions you enter.

faq.question: What is the difference between 16:9 and 21:9?
faq.answer: 16:9 is standard widescreen (used by most TVs and monitors), while 21:9 is ultrawide (used by cinema screens and ultrawide monitors). 21:9 gives about 33% more horizontal space, which is great for immersive gaming and multitasking.

faq.question: How do megapixels relate to aspect ratio?
faq.answer: Megapixels measure total resolution (width × height / 1,000,000) while aspect ratio describes the shape. A 12MP image could be 4000×3000 (4:3) or 4242×2828 (3:2) — same megapixels but different shapes. Aspect ratio determines framing; megapixels determine detail.

sections.title: What Is the Aspect Ratio Calculator?
sections.body: The Aspect Ratio Calculator is a free online tool designed for photographers who need quick, accurate calculations in the photography space. By entering your mode, width, height, you get instant results including aspect ratio, decimal ratio, dimensions. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting aspect ratio right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In photography, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by photographers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to capture better images with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your mode and need to find the right aspect ratio. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Mode
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your mode — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: width, height, aspect ratio, custom ratio, known dimension. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your aspect ratio, decimal ratio, dimensions. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your mode before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both aspect ratio and decimal ratio — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to capture better images.
If you're unsure about your known dimension, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
